source: NvChad - Plugins: Overview: how user plugins are organized, loaded, and the restart requirement

questions.q: User plugins are inserted from which folder?
questions.why: NvChad loads user plugins from lua/plugins.

questions.q: How are plugin files in the plugins folder loaded?
options: All .lua files in the folder are loaded and merged
One at a time in alphabetical order
questions.why: Every .lua file in plugins is queried and merged, so you can split them up.

questions.q: A plugin file must enclose its plugin configs within...
options: a Lua table returned with return { { ... }, }
questions.why: Return a Lua table containing the plugin tables.

options: use separate files for your own plugins (e.g. editor.lua) rather than piling into init.lua
questions.why: Separate files keep the configuration organized; init.lua is best for overrides.

questions.q: The configs folder is intended for...
options: particularly long or user-modifiable plugin settings (e.g. conform.nvim)
questions.why: Long or editable settings go in configs.

questions.q: After editing a plugins file, to get the plugin's full functionality you must...
options: stop nvim and reopen it (Neovim has no live auto-update of a running editor)
questions.why: Neovim must be restarted to fully load a newly added plugin.

questions.q: Which sites does the page suggest for researching Neovim plugins?
questions.why: Dotfyle and Neovimcraft catalog plugins and configs.

questions.q: The plugin-search feature that helps organize user plugins has been present since...
questions.why: Since v2.5 you can organize user plugins more efficiently.
